codingTopics: Streaming TTS audio generation with low latency (stream, streams, realtime, pipeline)
Text normalization and tokenization for speech (string, dictionary, prefix)
Audio buffer/ring management for playback (cache, memory, data structure, realtime)
Rate-limited API wrapper for model calls (systems, cache, sliding-window)
Practical product coding over realistic scenarios (array, string, systems)
systemDesignTopics: Design a low-latency streaming text-to-speech API that starts audio playback before full synthesis completes
Design a voice-cloning pipeline that trains and serves custom voices safely at scale
Design the model-serving layer batching concurrent TTS requests on GPUs under a latency SLO
Design a dubbing pipeline that transcribes, translates, and re-voices audio while preserving timing

coding: Build a rate-limited wrapper around a TTS model API that queues requests, respects a per-key quota, and streams audio chunks back as they're generated.
Given input text, implement text normalization (numbers, dates, abbreviations) into a form a TTS model can pronounce. Discuss edge cases per language.
Implement a ring buffer that smooths streamed audio chunks for gapless playback under jittery network delivery.
Process a stream of generated audio frames and detect/patch underruns so playback never stalls, keeping memory bounded.
Realistic scenario: synthesize speech for a long article. Design and code a chunking strategy that keeps first-audio latency low while preserving prosody across chunk boundaries.
behavioral: ElevenLabs wants 'founders who happen to be looking for a job.' Tell me about acting like an owner beyond your role.
Describe building a working product quickly from an ambiguous problem. How did you make judgment calls?
Tell me about shipping fast, learning from users, and iterating.
Describe taking full responsibility for an outcome that wasn't formally yours.
systemDesign: Design ElevenLabs' streaming TTS API delivering first audio in well under a second.
Design a safe voice-cloning training and serving pipeline.
Design GPU batching for concurrent TTS under a latency SLO.
Design a dubbing pipeline (transcribe, translate, re-voice) preserving timing.
values: ElevenLabs is pushing the frontier of AI voice. What draws you to that mission?
Voice cloning has real misuse risk. How do you weigh capability against safety?
What does high agency and founder mentality look like in your day-to-day work?
other: How does ElevenLabs compare to Deepgram or Descript?

ElevenLabs Interview Guide (2026): AI Voice Generation

On this page

- Process

- What they actually ask

- Prep priorities

- Frequently Asked Questions

Updated May 6, 2026 · techinterview.org

⏱ 1 min read

ElevenLabs
elevenlabs.io ↗

ElevenLabs is the leading AI voice-generation platform — TTS, voice cloning, dubbing, and a real-time conversational voice agent. Founded by ex-Google/Palantir engineers. Series C in 2024. The interview emphasizes ML systems for audio, low-latency real-time inference, and the engineering of multilingual voice products.

Process

Recruiter screen → 60-minute coding (Python or systems language) → onsite virtual: 2 coding, 1 ML system design, 1 craft deep-dive, 1 behavioral. ML/research candidates get a research deep-dive. Cycle: 3–5 weeks.

What they actually ask

- Design a real-time TTS streaming server with sub-300ms latency

- Design voice-cloning enrollment plus abuse-prevention safeguards

- Design a multilingual dubbing pipeline (ASR → MT → TTS) with style preservation

- Coding: medium-hard DSA, often ML-flavored

- Behavioral: ownership, taste, fast-moving startup

Levels and comp (2026)

- SE: $190K–$260K total (London bands £110K–£160K plus equity)

- Senior SE: $270K–$370K total (London bands £160K–£230K plus equity)

- Staff / ML Research: $380K–$560K+ total at top of band

Prep priorities

- Be fluent in Python (research/serving), C++/CUDA helpful for inference roles

- Understand TTS architectures (autoregressive vs diffusion), streaming inference, and audio codecs

- Brush up on ASR, alignment, and multilingual NLP

Frequently Asked Questions

Is ElevenLabs remote-friendly?

Hubs in London (HQ), New York, San Francisco. Many engineering roles hybrid; some senior+ roles remote.

How does ElevenLabs compare to Deepgram or Descript?

Deepgram is ASR-first. Descript is creator-tools-first. ElevenLabs is TTS/voice-generation leader and now expanding to conversational. Comp is competitive for AI startups at top of band.

What is the engineering culture?

Small, technically dense, taste-driven, fast-shipping. Strong product-research-engineering blend.
